description: Pluggable RailAdapter registry — admin API to add/swap rails, routing policy engine that selects a rail per payment by amount/region/merchant

actors.name: Payments Gateway
actors.description: Caller — asks registry to route each payment

actors.name: Admin operator
actors.description: Registers / swaps rail adapters via admin API

actors.name: RailAdapter plugin
actors.description: Implementation of the rail contract (PayShap, Visa, RTGS, sandbox, …)

fields.name: adapter_module
fields.label: Module path implementing the RailAdapter contract

fields.name: priority
fields.label: Tie-break priority when policies match multiple rails

fields.name: enabled
fields.label: Whether the rail is currently routable

contract: MUST: every rail adapter implements authorize(), capture(), refund(), status(), supports_currency()
MUST: registering a rail does NOT require device firmware updates
routing: MUST: routing evaluates policies in priority order; first match wins
MUST: disabled rails are never selected even when policy matches
admin: MUST: rail registration requires operator role + mTLS-authenticated admin session

anti_patterns.rule: Do not hardcode rail selection in PGW — always delegate to the registry
anti_patterns.why: Required by rail-registry contract — violating this rule breaks security, privacy, or correctness guarantees documented in rules{}

anti_patterns.rule: Do not accept adapter_module paths outside the allowlist — arbitrary code load risk
anti_patterns.why: Required by rail-registry contract — violating this rule breaks security, privacy, or correctness guarantees documented in rules{}

given: adapter_module is in the signed allowlist
rail_registered.result: Rail available for routing on next payment

given: admin session missing or insufficient role

errors.message: A rail with that id already exists

related.reason: PGW is the sole caller of the registry during routing
Mock rail used for sandbox and demos
